description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:git Docker image with a docker:dind service and a before_script that echoes a login message and runs docker login with $CI_REGISTRY and $CI_BUILD_TOKEN. Define a single job named build_image that runs only on the master branch. In that job execute the exact shell commands: echo "Building from $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE", docker build -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_BUILD_REF}" . | tee build.log, VERSION=$(grep "^VERSION=" build.log | cut -d"=" -f2), echo "GCC ${VERSION}", echo "tagging ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_BUILD_REF} => ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${VERSION}", docker tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_BUILD_REF}" "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${VERSION}", docker push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${VERSION}", docker tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${VERSION}" "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest", docker push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest". No caches, artifacts, or deployment steps are defined.
